food_subgroup: Catered sharing platter — high tea assortment including finger sandwiches, pastries, scones, and bite-sized savoury and sweet items

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a high tea catering set. FOODON_03315116 (food product mixture) is used as the closest parent category for a multi-component assorted food platter.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (composite food — mixed dish) is the closest defensible parent for a multi-item catered high tea set. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for high tea assortments.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to a catered high tea set platter. SNOMED CT is not applicable here.

serving.description: 1 set for 8–10 persons (approximately 1,800–2,200 g total platter weight)
serving.natural_language_portion: One realistic serving is approximately 200 g per person, representing one individual portion drawn from the shared high tea platter of assorted finger sandwiches, scones, pastries, and savoury bites.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite nutrient profile for assorted high tea platter — derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for finger sandwiches, plain scones, cream pastries, and mixed savoury bites
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a catered high tea set. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of typical high tea components: finger sandwiches (~35% by weight, USDA SR Legacy 18267 bread + deli fillings), plain scones (~25%, USDA SR Legacy 18023), cream-filled pastries (~20%, USDA SR Legacy 18337), and assorted savoury bites (~20%, USDA SR Legacy 21376). Per-100g values represent the blended composite. Confidence is low due to high variability across restaurant preparations.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed refined-flour baked goods and sandwiches — analogous estimate based on white bread GI (75) and plain scone GI (92) weighted composite; Atkinson FS et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values, Diabetes Care
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a catered high tea set. GI estimated at 62 using a weighted composite of approved GI values: white bread sandwiches GI 75 (University of Sydney GI Database), plain scones GI 92 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care), cream pastries GI ~55 (fat-dampened refined flour), and savoury bites GI ~45 (mixed protein-fat-carbohydrate). Weighted by approximate platter proportions (35% sandwiches, 25% scones, 20% pastries, 20% savoury). GL per 200 g serving = (62 × 36.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 45.6, rounded to 45.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~620 mg per 200 g serving) from processed deli fillings, savoury bites, and baked goods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small plate and avoid high-sodium savoury items within the set, as a single 200 g serving may contribute approximately 27% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~45.5 per 200 g serving) driven by refined-flour scones, white-bread sandwiches, and sugar-rich pastries
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this set due to its high glycemic load; selecting protein-rich finger sandwiches and limiting scones and cream pastries can help moderate postprandial blood glucose excursions, and portion control to 100–120 g is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.8 g per 100 g) and trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g) from cream, butter, and pastry components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate intake of cream-filled pastries and buttery scones within the set, as saturated fat content may contribute to LDL elevation; choosing sandwich components over pastry items and limiting total serving to 100–150 g is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mini High Tea Set (8–10 pax) is a catered sharing platter designed for group occasions in Singapore, typically comprising an assortment of finger sandwiches, plain and cream scones, bite-sized pastries, mini cakes, and savoury bites, served alongside tea. This format is rooted in the British afternoon tea tradition adapted to the Singaporean catering context, where such sets are popular for corporate events, birthday celebrations, and social gatherings. The platter is assembled and presented on tiered stands or serving trays, with items baked or prepared fresh by the catering kitchen. Texturally, the set offers contrast between soft crustless sandwiches, crumbly buttery scones, flaky pastry shells, and crisp savoury bites. Flavour profiles range from savoury (smoked salmon, egg mayo, chicken fillings) to sweet (cream, jam, fruit tarts, chocolate). Nutritionally, the set is energy-dense at approximately 285 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (7.2 g), significant fat (12.5 g, of which 6.8 g saturated), and high refined carbohydrate content (36.8 g) with notable sugar (12.4 g). The glycemic load per 200 g serving is high (~45.5), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for those with hypertension. The set is not suitable for gluten-free, vegan, or dairy-free diets. It is commonly ordered from premium catering outlets such as Kazo Premium in Singapore and is available via food delivery platforms including GrabFood Singapore.

How many people does a mini high tea set serve?

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or equivalent) contains a direct entry for a catered high tea set platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for individual component food types. Actual nutritional content varies substantially across restaurants and preparation styles. GI is an analogous estimate from approved GI sources. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated composites; actual values will vary significantly depending on the specific items included by the restaurant (Kazo Premium - Funan) and portion distribution within the platter. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate. 5. Glycemic index set to 62 (medium) as an analogous weighted estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care; no direct GI measurement exists for a catered high tea set. 6. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Beverages', which is incorrect for a multi-component food platter; corrected to 'Mixed' per content. 7. Halal status is unconfirmed for Kazo Premium - Funan; dietary_flags.is_halal set to false pending verification. 8. Price SGD 88.00 per set for 8–10 persons (GrabFood Singapore, Kazo Premium - Funan). 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite components and carry low confidence.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from 'Beverages' (input error) to 'Mixed' to reflect the multi-component catered platter nature of the food.
Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singaporean cuisine.
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency check — all aliases_by_language entries verified present in flat aliases list.
Estimated composite nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy component entries with full methodology documentation.
Derived analogous GI estimate (62, medium) from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care with weighted-average methodology documented.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit reasons in notes.
Set ontology codes as closest_parent with gap notes; used only allowed match_type values (exact, closest_parent, none).
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance; used 'Type 2 Diabetes' as human_condition_name.
Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full food identity, preparation, texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and nutrient profile.

Revised documentation for -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in the 'notes' field to explicitly state 'not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries' for clearer compliance with g3_numeric_hygiene rule.
